calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-11-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Breather Products Us, Inc. Memorandum Of Points Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Dec-06-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for entry of default judgment against Defendant Breather Products US, Inc.

Dept. 514; Judge Harry Jacobs; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:12:29 a.m. and concluding at 09:17:53 a.m.

Plaintiff CPH 564, LP a California Limited Partnership represented by Elizabeth M. Pappy, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Breather Products US, Inc. not appearing.

KAYLIN ALEXANDER is placed under oath.

Testimony presented on October 04, 2022 and Plaintiff Exhibit Nos. A thru F were MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE.
Court ordered matter continued after the presentation of evidence for Counsel for Plaintiff to file a detailed declaration re: its request for attorney fees.
Declaration of Elizabeth M. Pappy in Support of Request for Entry of Court Judgment filed October 19, 2022 addressing its request for attorney fees.

The matter is submitted.
By the statements made by Plaintiff's witness and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff CPH 564, LP, a California Limited Partnership, and against Defendant Breather Products US, Inc., as follows:

1. Damages: $3,126,888.34
2. Costs: $975.25

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $159,360.27

Proposed judgment to be submitted.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-26-2022 Continued To Jan-24-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Breather Products Us, Inc. Memorandum Of Points Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-04-2022 Continued To Dec-06-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for motion for entry of default judgment against Defendant Breather Products US, Inc.

Dept. 514; Judge Murlene J. Randle; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:42:48 a.m. and concluding at 11:21:13 a.m.

Plaintiff CPH 564, LP a California Limited Partnership represented by Kelsey Eisen, Esq.
Defendant Breather Products US, Inc. not appearing.

KAYLIN ALEXANDRA is placed under oath and testies.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit A - Breather's unpaid charges for suite 300
Plaintiff's Exhibit B - Breather's unpaid charges for suite 316
Plaintiff's Exhibit C - Breather's unpaid charges for suite 301
Plaintiff's Exhibit D - Breather's unpaid charges for suite 150
Plaintiff's Exhibit E - Breather's unpaid charges for suite 401
Plaintiff's Exhibit F - Summary spreadsheet containing calculationsfor Future Projections (losses)

Plaintiff's Exhibit G - Calcualtions for both past rent already collected and expected future rent from new tenants
Plaintiff's Exhibit K - Notice of Termination of all Leases Involving Breather Products US Inc.

Plaintiff's Exhibits A-F are ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE

Court finds that Plaintiff Counsel's Declaration is not sufficient to support it's request for attorney's fees and it's proposed judgment is not on the mandatory judicial counsil form.

Counsel for Plaintiff shall file a declaration detailing it's work on this case to support it's request for attorney fees and submit a proposed judgment on the mandatory judicial counsil form.

Court orders matter continued to Dec-06-2022 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 514 for final ruling. Plaintiff's claims have been summarized and tesimony taken. The admission of Plaintiff's Exhibits G and K pending.
